paper_title: Materials and Methods of Geological and Geochemical Analysis

methods.method_name: Ash Sample Preparation and SEM-EDS Analysis
methods.description: This method describes the preparation of ash samples for analysis and the use of Scanning Electron Microscopy with Energy-Dispersive Spectrometry (SEM-EDS) to analyze glass shards in the samples.

steps.description: The ash samples were wrapped in plastic bags and transported to the laboratory. Then they were dried at room temperature, observed and described under a binocular microscope.
steps.searchable_summary: Dry and observe ash samples under a microscope

steps.description: A fraction of the bulk samples was dried and embedded in epoxy resin, coated with graphite and screened for glass shards using a scanning electron microscope (SEM).
steps.searchable_summary: Embed samples in resin, coat with graphite, and screen using SEM

steps.description: Energy-dispersive spectrometry (EDS) of glass shards was performed using an EDAX-DX micro-analyzer mounted on a Philips SEM 515.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform EDS analysis on glass shards using SEM

methods.description: This method involves using Inductively Coupled Plasma Mass Spectrometry (ICP-MS) to determine the concentration of various elements in a bulk sample.

steps.description: The complete sample dissolution was obtained by repeated treatment with concentrated HF+HNO3 on a hotplate.
steps.searchable_summary: Dissolve samples using HF and HNO3 on a hotplate

steps.description: The concentrations of various elements were determined by ICP-MS at the University of Pisa.

methods.description: This method describes the process of determining the Sr isotope composition using thermal ionization mass spectrometry.

steps.description: 0.2 g of sample were leached several times with cold 6 N HCl for 10 min, and then rinsed in pure Milli Q® H2O.
steps.searchable_summary: Leach sample with HCl and rinse with Milli Q water

steps.description: After dissolution, Sr was separated by conventional ion-exchange chromatographic techniques.
Sr isotope composition was determined by thermal ionization mass spectrometry.
steps.searchable_summary: Determine Sr isotope composition using mass spectrometry

methods.description: This method involves analyzing the grain size distribution of a sample using a Beckman Coulter Multisizer.

steps.description: Grain-size analyses were carried out between 3.5 and 9 φ at 0.5 φ intervals using a Beckman Coulter Multisizer 4.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform grain-size analysis using Beckman Coulter Multisizer

steps.description: The median size (Mdφ) and the sorting parameters of each grain-size distribution were calculated using a Fortran 90 code.
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate median size and sorting parameters using Fortran code

methods.description: This method describes the process of cleaning and preparing a sample for radiocarbon dating and calibrating the results.

steps.description: The sample was cleaned in an ultrasonic bath with the addition of oxygen peroxide and then gently etched with diluted HCl.
steps.searchable_summary: Clean sample using ultrasonic bath and HCl etching

steps.description: Radiocarbon measurement was carried out and calibrated using the Marine13 curve.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform radiocarbon dating and calibrate using Marine13 curve
